_meta.purpose: Image-edit pass — within-tier from Knight (L8) to Knight Champion (L9, Tier 3 top). Apply ADDITIVE upgrades — closed visor extending downward from the L8 half-visor (face fully concealed); backswept fins on kettle helm sides; gold trim on pennon + gold ring at lance socket; steel crinet (moved from L8 per AD detail-economy); steel flanchards (plain — NO heraldic-stamp surface ornament).
_meta.input_image: image_urls[0] = UnitProgression/Cavalry/out/v1/L8/composite/cavalry_L8_v1_composited.png ; image_urls[1] = UnitProgression/Cavalry/Refs/L1_Base.png (scale anchor) — placeholder keeper version, user will rewire to actual keeper at runtime.
history: v1 (2026-05-07): initial author — Knight Champion (Tier 3 top) within-tier with 5 ADDITIVE adds per CLAUDE.md § Part 2 § K0 + AD FeedbackV0 detail-economy. (1) Closed visor extending the L8 half-visor downward (face fully concealed — no skin / hair / eyes / mustache visible above the bevor). (2) Backswept fins on kettle helm sides (~5 inches tall, swept back over the helm tail). (3) Gold-trimmed pennon + gold ring at lance socket. (4) Steel crinet on horse neck (moved from L8 to L9 per AD detail-economy — pacing the horse-barding ramp after the rider's L8 boot capstone). (5) Steel flanchards on horse flanks — PLAIN steel with riveted edges only (NO stamped heraldic shape per AD detail-economy).
v3 (2026-05-07): pipeline update — drop L1_Base scale anchor from image_urls and prompt body. Single-image input (prior-level composited keeper only) per updated pipeline + docs.
v4 (2026-05-07): scrub belt-studs PRESERVE/negative references — L2 IRON STUDS beat dropped per AD detail-economy.
v6 (2026-05-07): added prophylactic anti-rivet/stud guardrail negative — same as Infantry chain, applied through the Cavalry chain after rivet bleed-through observed at L3 generation.

v7 (2026-05-07): scrub stale "3-lame layered pauldrons" PRESERVE references — L4-L10 pauldron progression now reads as single continuous piece (L4 disc → L6 thicker dome → L8 dome + bicep extension → L10 dome+extension grown in volume). No stacked-plate construction language anywhere in the chain.

v8 (2026-05-07): retone all metal-color language from cool silver-gray to warm silver-gray matching the rendered L1 kettle-helm tone — cool/blueish steel was reading distinctly different from the L1 baseline's warm gray, getting more pronounced at higher levels. Added negative clause locking out cool/blue-gray steel palette.

v9 (2026-05-07): scrub stale chest-band PRESERVE/negative references — L6 chest-band-above-breastplate was removed (user feedback: cloth-above-armor looks impractical). Cuirass now sits flush against the bevor; only the skirt band of livery remains on the rider.
v10 (2026-05-07): retone metal language from 'warm gray' -> 'neutral gray' (prior pass overshot warm; balanced gray with neither blue nor brown shadow tint matches the L1 baseline most accurately).

v11 (2026-05-07): scrub model-invisible context from prompt body — removed L<n> level-tag references (model is stateless and cannot resolve our level naming), authoring-decision parentheticals (LOCKED/REVISED/REMOVED dates, per AD FeedbackV0, etc.), and cross-level range refs. All persistent decisions remain in _meta.history + the progression plan + CLAUDE.md. See Archer/CLAUDE.md § Part 3 locked decision #27 (drift-corrected: residual parenthetical level-tags also dropped).

prompt: EDIT GOAL — Apply FIVE visible ADDITIVE upgrades to the mounted cavalry knight in the FIRST input image, marking the within-tier promotion from Knight to Knight Champion. Every the levels above detail must remain visible at this level. The rider already wears the existing Knight kit: kettle helm with backswept neck-guard skirt, brim reinforcement, additional brim rivets, half-visor extending downward from the brim covering forehead-to-nose-bridge with horizontal eye slit, and a buttery polished yellow gold trim band wrapping the brim line; mustache visible below the half-visor's lower edge (the new closed visor at conceals the mustache — see Edit Goal item 1); steel bevor at the throat; full steel cuirass with medial ridge and 1 simple horizontal abdominal seam; skirt band of yellow+red livery (narrowed at this level) — the cuirass sits flush against the bevor (no chest band); full-arm scale-mail sleeves bicep-to-wrist; single-piece steel pauldrons on both shoulders (thicker domes + bicep coverage extension, no stacked-plate construction); steel vambraces on both forearms layered on top of the scale mail; steel finger-plates + clean knuckle disc layered on top of bare-hand silhouettes (with leather grip cord still visible between gauntleted fingers and wooden haft); leather waist belt with plain leather belt and brass buckle; longer 2-wider-lame steel tassets at both hips covering mid-thigh; full steel sabatons on both boots; blue trousers; brown leather riding boots in stirrups (calf and heel still leather above the sabaton); wooden spear haft (warm-brown wood, with iron pommel cap, leather grip cord, iron banding rings + reinforced socket, steel leaf-tip head + red+yellow heraldic pennon); hip pouch on spear-side hip (the only belt accessory). The horse already wears: full steel chamfron with forehead spike, iron-buckled bridle with iron browband, iron banding on saddle pommel and cantle, iron peytral plate beneath the red+blue checkered caparison. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — no white markings of any kind. CRITICAL SPATIAL ANCHOR — treat 'spear-side' as the side of the rider's body / canvas where the upright wooden spear haft is visible (the side closer to the horse's tail), and 'rein-side' as the OPPOSITE side. Do NOT use 'right' or 'left' to interpret placement. (1) CLOSED VISOR EXTENDING THE HALF-VISOR DOWNWARD (additive — kettle base preserved underneath) — extend the existing half-visor downward to fully close the face. The new lower visor section attaches at the existing half-visor's lower edge and extends down to cover the nose, mouth, and chin all the way to the bevor's upper edge. The new visor section adds two small breathing perforations (~0.25 inch diameter circular vents) at the mouth area and continues the horizontal eye slit from the existing half-visor (the eye slit remains the only opening for vision). The face is now FULLY CONCEALED — no skin, no hair, no eyes, no mustache, no expression visible above the bevor. The kettle helm's domed CROWN still remains visible above the closed visor; the visor pivots from the kettle's brim seam (same attachment). The gold trim band at the kettle's brim line remains visible above the closed visor. Cool silver-gray steel matching the kettle and half-visor. (2) BACKSWEPT FINS / WINGS ADDED TO THE KETTLE HELM SIDES — keep the kettle helm + visor (now closed at this level) + neck-guard skirt. ADD a pair of small upward-and-backward-curving STEEL FINS rising from the temple regions of the kettle, one fin on each side. Each fin is approximately 5 inches tall, swept upward and backward over the kettle's domed crown and tail (the fin's leading edge at the temple, the trailing edge curving back over the helm's neck-guard skirt). Cool silver-gray steel matching the kettle. The fins are pure additions — they sit ON TOP of the kettle's domed crown and do not pierce or replace the crown. Each fin attaches at the temple with a small riveted plate. (3) GOLD TRIM ON PENNON + GOLD RING AT LANCE SOCKET — the existing red+yellow heraldic pennon gains: (a) a buttery polished yellow gold TRIM BAND running along the pennon's outer edges (top, leading edge, trailing edge, and the forked tail), ~0.3 inch wide; (b) a small buttery gold TASSEL at the trailing tip of the forked tail (~1 inch long). Also ADD a thin buttery gold RING wrapping the lance haft at the SOCKET where the existing steel leaf-tip head meets the wooden haft (a clean ~0.4 inch tall gold band wrapping the haft just below the socket). All gold renders as BUTTERY POLISHED YELLOW GOLD METAL — saturated warm-yellow contrasting strongly against the steel and the wood — NOT brass, NOT bronze, NOT yellow paint, NOT mustard tint. (4) STEEL CRINET ON THE HORSE'S NECK — add articulated neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) plates running along the back of the horse's neck from the rear edge of the existing chamfron down to the front of the saddle, covering the upper portion of the neck where the mane sits. The crinet is built from approximately 5 stacked horizontal lames, each lame ~1.5 inch tall and following the curve of the neck, joined at the seams by a single row of small dark-iron rivets per seam edge. The horse's dark brown mane is visible underneath the crinet as a thin line at the seam between adjacent lames. The crinet attaches at the rear of the chamfron (where it grows from the chamfron's neck-side edge) and at the front of the saddle (where its lower edge meets the saddle pommel). (5) STEEL FLANCHARDS ON THE HORSE'S FLANKS — PLAIN STEEL (NO heraldic stamp) — add articulated neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) side plates protecting the horse's flanks, one flanchard on each side of the horse. Each flanchard is built from 2 wider stacked vertical lames, ~10 inches tall and ~8 inches wide, slightly curved to follow the horse's flank, with visible riveted edges (a single row of small dark-iron rivets along the upper edge where the flanchard attaches to the saddle girth, and a single row along the seam between the two lames). The flanchards REPLACE the lower-rear panels of the caparison's drape on each flank — the caparison's CENTRAL panel covering the horse's spine and rump remains visible between the two flanchards on the rump. CRITICAL — NO STAMPED HERALDIC SHAPE at the lower corner of the flanchard (no embossed cross, no embossed lion, no embossed coat-of-arms). The flanchards are PLAIN STEEL with riveted edges as the only surface detail. ===== PRESERVE FROM THE INPUT IMAGE EXACTLY ===== every other element of the FIRST input image must remain identical in shape, color, position, and proportion. THE RIDER FACE: NOT VISIBLE at this level — the new closed visor fully conceals the face. NO skin, NO hair, NO eyes, NO mustache visible above the bevor. (The mustache and face are still implicitly preserved underneath the closed visor — they are simply not rendered on top of the visor surface.) THE RIDER HEAD COVERING: the the levels above kettle helm with all its accumulated additions (backswept neck-guard skirt + brim reinforcement + additional brim rivets, half-visor at the upper portion of the face, buttery gold trim band at the brim line) — unchanged at this level. The new closed visor extends downward from the existing half-visor per Edit Goal item (1). The new backswept fins are added to the kettle's temples per Edit Goal item (2). THE RIDER NECK: the existing steel bevor / collar plate at the throat, unchanged. THE RIDER TORSO: the existing reinforced cuirass with medial ridge + 1 horizontal abdominal seam — unchanged; the cuirass flush against the bevor (NO chest band of cloth above the cuirass); the skirt band of yellow+red livery between cuirass bottom edge and tassets top edge (narrow but still visible); the wide brown leather waist belt with brass buckle (plain leather, no studs). THE RIDER ARMS AND HANDS: the EXISTING FULL-ARM scale-mail sleeves; the EXISTING single-piece steel pauldrons on both shoulders (single continuous piece of shaped steel, + +, unchanged); the EXISTING steel vambraces on the forearms (unchanged); the EXISTING steel finger-plates + clean knuckle disc layered on top of the bare-hand silhouettes; both hands gripping the wooden spear haft (spear-side) and the brown leather reins (rein-side). THE RIDER LEGS: dark blue / muted steel-blue knee-length trousers visible at the calf below the existing full sabatons; chunky warm-brown leather riding boots — body of each boot still visible at the calf above the sabaton and at the heel; the EXISTING full steel sabatons on both boots (unchanged); both boots in stirrups; EXISTING longer 2-wider-lame steel tassets at both hips covering mid-thigh (unchanged). THE WEAPON: the wooden spear haft warm-brown wood (the levels above unchanged) with the existing iron pommel cap, leather grip cord, iron banding rings + reinforced socket, steel leaf-tip head, and red+yellow heraldic pennon — all preserved. The new gold trim on pennon + gold tassel + gold ring at lance socket are added per Edit Goal item (3). THE REINS: the brown leather reins at the horse's withers, gripped relaxed by the gauntleted rein-side hand. THE HORSE: the SOLID chestnut-brown chibi horse facing the viewer's RIGHT — head and muzzle on the rein-side, rump and tail on the spear-side, four-square stance, neck arched, ears forward. THE HORSE IS SOLID CHESTNUT BROWN ACROSS THE ENTIRE BODY — NO WHITE MARKINGS — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling. Mane visible only as a thin line between the new crinet's lames; tail dark brown extending behind the rump. THE HORSE TACK: the brown leather saddle with iron banding on pommel and cantle; the iron-buckled bridle with iron browband; the existing full steel chamfron with forehead spike — UNCHANGED at this level; the existing iron peytral plate beneath the caparison; the new steel crinet running along the back of the neck per Edit Goal item (4); the new steel flanchards on both flanks per Edit Goal item (5). THE CAPARISON: the large rectangular cloth caparison with red+blue checkered pattern and steel-blue trim band — at the lower-rear panels of the caparison on each flank are now COVERED / REPLACED by the new steel flanchards; the CENTRAL panel of the caparison covering the horse's spine and rump REMAINS visible between the two flanchards. THE EXISTING the levels above KIT: ALL preserved unchanged in shape, color, and position. NO knife sheath at any hip (removed at). The hip pouch remains as the only belt accessory. ===== POSE (must match the input image exactly) ===== Three-quarter view, the rider's body angled approximately 15-25 degrees toward the viewer's left. The HORSE faces toward the viewer's RIGHT. Rider sits upright in the saddle with both feet in stirrups, slight forward lean. THE SPEAR ARM (the rider's arm on the spear-side of the frame) is bent at the elbow with the upper arm vertical and the forearm angled slightly forward; the gauntleted hand grips the wooden spear haft at the existing leather grip cord wrap. THE REIN ARM (the rider's arm on the rein-side of the frame) is extended forward and slightly down, elbow bent at roughly 110 degrees; the gauntleted hand grips the brown leather reins at the level of the horse's withers / shoulder. The pose reads as a calm mounted patrol stance, with a clearly mightier silhouette than — face concealed by closed visor, fins extending vertical extent of the helm, horse neck and flanks now armored. NOT a charge, NOT a couched-lance attack, NOT a rear, NOT a pivot. ===== STYLE / OUTLINE ===== Supercell / Kingshot stylized mobile game art, hand-painted digital illustration with soft painted shading and visible brushwork, saturated rich colors, warm shadow tones never gray, soft painted highlights on metal and leather. Clean thin black silhouette outline around the entire rider+horse+spear+caparison silhouette in deep warm brown-black. Interior linework remains soft and value-driven, never hard black. ===== FRAMING / SCALE (must match these canonical numbers exactly — with small vertical extent acceptable from the existing helm fins) ===== 1024x1024 square canvas. Rider+horse silhouette occupies approximately 77.6-79% of canvas vertical height (small upward growth acceptable from the new helm fins). The wooden spear haft extends UP past the helm with the existing steel leaf-tip near or at the canvas top edge. DO NOT zoom in, DO NOT shrink, DO NOT crop the helm fins or hooves. Clean opaque WHITE background — no light gray tint, no off-white shading. ===== NEGATIVE — DO NOT ===== DO NOT replace the kettle helm with a sallet, closed helm, conical helm, or any other helm shape — the kettle STAYS at this level; the closed visor extends downward from the existing half-visor / from the kettle's brim, the kettle's domed crown remains visible above the closed visor and between the new fins; DO NOT show the rider's face at this level — it is fully concealed by the closed visor (no skin, no eyes, no mustache visible above the bevor); DO NOT add a tall plumed crest at this level (that arrives at this level); DO NOT make the fins gold — they are neutral silver-gray steel matching the existing kettle-helm tone (balanced gray, neither blue-shadowed nor brown-shadowed) only at this level (gold spike caps arrive at this level); DO NOT make the fins taller than ~5 inches at this level (they grow taller); DO NOT add helm horns or animal-themed motifs to the kettle; DO NOT add gold trim, gold piping, gold rivets, or gold filigree on the cuirass / pauldrons / vambraces / tassets / sabatons / chamfron / crinet / flanchards at this level — the only gold is the pennon trim + tassel + lance socket ring (the kettle helm gold band from also remains visible); DO NOT stamp a heraldic shape (cross, lion, eagle, coat-of-arms) on the flanchards — the flanchards are PLAIN steel with riveted edges only; DO NOT add a heraldic emblem to the caparison or any other surface at this level; DO NOT replace the existing full chamfron — it stays unchanged at this level; DO NOT add gold-rosette boss to the chamfron at this level (that arrives at this level); DO NOT extend the crinet over the horse's withers or onto the saddle — the crinet ends at the saddle pommel; DO NOT extend the crinet to cover the horse's mane entirely — the mane should still be visible as a thin line between the crinet's lames; DO NOT use chainmail / fine ring mesh / interlocking metal rings on the crinet — it is articulated steel plates; DO NOT shrink the existing longer tassets back to length; DO NOT break the tassets into 3 or more lames at this level — exactly 2 wider lames per tasset; DO NOT replace the wooden spear haft with a steel haft — the wooden haft STAYS warm-brown wood with all its accumulated kit; DO NOT erase the existing leather grip cord, iron banding rings, reinforced socket, steel leaf-tip head, or red+yellow heraldic pennon; DO NOT make the entire pennon gold — only the trim band + tassel + lance socket ring are gold; the pennon body remains red+yellow split heraldic; DO NOT add a knife sheath, dagger sheath, dagger, or any second belt accessory; DO NOT change the horse coat color from solid chestnut brown; DO NOT add any white markings on the horse — no white blaze, no white star, no white socks, no white speckling; DO NOT change the horse's pose; DO NOT make the horse rear, walk, gallop, pivot, or move; DO NOT change the horse to a pony, hobby-horse, wooden cutout horse, donkey, mule, or any other species; DO NOT replace the iron peytral plate beneath the caparison — it remains; DO NOT remove the central panel of the caparison between the two new flanchards; DO NOT change the caparison's red+blue checkered pattern or steel-blue trim band; DO NOT add iron rivets, studs, embossed dots, or any small metal punctuation along the leather waist belt, the leather under-jacket sleeves, the surcoat hem, the saddle leather, the bridle straps, the caparison panels, the rider's hands, or any surface beyond what the input image already shows; DO NOT add metal banding rings or riveted hardware appearing as decoration on the kettle-helm body, the surcoat, or the wooden spear haft middle (the existing kettle's existing brim rivets are functional construction rivets and stay unchanged); DO NOT change the chibi proportions; DO NOT change the camera angle or composition; DO NOT crop the helm fins or hooves; DO NOT change the background, add scenery, or add a ground plane; DO NOT make the background transparent, checkerboard-patterned, light-gray, off-white, or any non-white color; DO NOT drift the art style; DO NOT add a glow, rim light, blue rim light, cyan rim light, or any new light source; DO NOT change the aspect ratio from 1:1 square.
